leadText: Tadbulb (Japanese: ズピカ Zupika) is an Electric-type Pokémon introduced in Generation IX. It evolves into Bellibolt when exposed to a Thunder Stone.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Tadbulb is a small amphibian Pokémon with a spherical yellow head that takes up most of its body. This head has two white spots at the top and can emit light, and Tadbulb blinks it on and off to warn its allies. On the bottom of the head is Tadbulb's face, which is black with beady yellow eyes, a simple mouth with orange lips, and yellow circles on its cheeks. Tadbulb's head and tail are separated by copper-colored discs below the head. Tadbulb's tail is black with a U-shaped copper-colored pattern at the tip, and is used by Tadbulb to generate electricity by shaking it. Tadbulb can generate and store enough electricity to power cities such as Levincia . It is also capable of using electricity to float in the air; if the weather is stormy, it can float at a higher altitude. Tadbulb are known to inhabit wetlands and riverbanks. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Tadbulb evolves into Bellibolt . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Tadbulb Electric → First Evolution Bellibolt Electric

Generation IX Paldea #170 Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #— Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Legends: Z-A . Scarlet Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies. Violet It floats using the electricity stored in its body. When thunderclouds are around, Tadbulb will float higher off the ground. Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Contents 1 Trivia 1.1 Concept and development 1.2 Origin 1.2.1 Name origin Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Tadbulb is the only Pokémon with a base stat total of 272. Concept and development [ edit | edit source ] Tadbulb and its Evolution were designed by Atsuko Nishida . [ 1 ] Origin [ edit | edit source ] Tadbulb appears to be based on a tadpole and a light bulb . Name origin [ edit | edit source ] Tadbulb may be a combination of tadpole and light bulb . Zupika may be a combination of 頭 zu (head) and ピカピカ pikapika (onomatopoeia for sparkling). ↑ Instagram post by Atsuko Nishida (@coloatsu) on the Paldean Pokémon designed by her. Posted on December 1, 2024.
